commands.author: brokemac79

target.title: Proposal: harden ClawSweeper automerge pass-marker head contract
target.body:
## Summary

I prepared a Clownfish-side implementation to harden the ClawSweeper -> Clownfish automerge pass-marker contract, but I cannot open a PR from my account because the upstream repository currently grants me read-only permission.

Implementation branch: https://github.com/brokemac79/clownfish/tree/codex/automerge-contract-harness

Current branch head: https://github.com/brokemac79/clownfish/commit/9aefc5329a4cb7fec0908c8025174e7ec39ad35e

Current upstream base: https://github.com/openclaw/clownfish/commit/fb60292bc7c11acbc6ef6a247bfdc667b70d970e

Current compare state: 1 commit ahead, 0 behind, with only the intended four files changed.

## What the change does functionally

The proposed change makes Clownfish treat a ClawSweeper pass marker as mergeable only when the marker carries the exact full PR head SHA that was reviewed.

It covers these cases:

- missing or `unknown` marker SHA stays non-mergeable
- shortened or malformed marker SHA stays non-mergeable
- stale marker SHA stays non-mergeable
- exact full 40-character reviewed-head SHA can proceed through the existing automerge path
- blocked automerge responses explain the exact reason instead of failing opaquely
- merge argument construction refuses to build `gh pr merge` arguments from a non-full SHA

The intended result is that Clownfish never falls back from a trusted ClawSweeper pass marker to an unpinned merge.

## Why this seems like a good Clownfish addition

ClawSweeper emits the review verdict marker, but Clownfish owns the router path that consumes trusted bot markers, classifies automerge readiness, renders why-not-merge output, and invokes `gh pr merge`. That makes Clownfish the right side to enforce the consumer contract.

This is meant to turn the exact-head part of the ClawSweeper/Clownfish automerge boundary into deterministic regression coverage, following the recent automerge work around #120 and the #121 through #128 cluster.

## Files changed on the prepared branch

- `scripts/comment-router-core.mjs`: adds shared full-SHA validation and reviewed-head blocker logic; refuses non-full SHAs when building merge args
- `scripts/comment-router.mjs`: uses the shared blocker in both automerge pass classification and final readiness validation
- `test/comment-router-core.test.mjs`: adds contract tests for missing, unknown, shortened, stale, and exact reviewed-head markers plus blocked response text
- `docs/auto-update-prs.md`: documents that pass-marker `sha` must be the full 40-character reviewed PR head

## Verification already run

After rebasing the prepared branch onto current `main`, I reran:

- focused router tests: 32/32 passing
- syntax checks for the touched router scripts
- `git diff --check`
- Linux container `npm run validate`: validated 6175 jobs
- Linux container `npm test`: 209 passing, 0 failing, 11 skipped due to optional `sqlite3` dependency not being available in the container

Earlier Codex review loop:

- `codex -c service_tier='fast' review --uncommitted` -> no actionable regressions found in the modified paths

## Handoff ask

Could a maintainer review the prepared branch and decide whether to open it as a PR, cherry-pick the commit, or adjust the shape before landing? Push access has been offered on the fork to the two maintainers most likely to want to amend the branch directly.
target.author: brokemac79

commands.reason: matching ClawSweeper response comment already exists
